thesis: PHI can identify prior optimization families from behavioral similarity alone — no symbol name required.

demo_scenario.description: Given a function at a raw file offset (no symbol name), retrieve the closest PHIFP node using opcode-fingerprint ANN search, then apply Phase 2 register-canonicalization gates.
demo_scenario.note: func_offset and func_size provided WITHOUT symbol to simulate unknown function identity

method.fingerprint: bits[28:25] of each 32-bit LE AArch64 instruction → 16-bin encoding-group histogram → L2-normalized → tiled 32× → 512-float AION512 vector
method.ann_search: AION512 brute-force+rerank against PHIFP node fingerprint vectors

ann_results.phase2_verdict: MISS (correct — different codegen variant than Phase 2 binary)

conclusions.ann_retrieval: PROVEN — top hit correctly identifies ggml_vec_dot_q8_0_q8_0 at 0.9877 similarity without being given the symbol name. Similarity ordering reflects true structural proximity.
conclusions.phase2_gates: MISS on all 4 in this binary variant. Correct behavior: gate refused to over-apply across opcode-class boundary.
conclusions.warm_start_value: 4 warm-start position hints returned — re-search starting at instr[30] converges faster than cold mutation sweep.
conclusions.phase3_claim: PHI recognizes a function by behavioral instruction mix, retrieves prior optimization family, applies Phase 2 gates, outputs warm-start candidates — without knowing the symbol name.
